# Free Retro

Free Retro is a real-time collaborative retrospective board. Create an unlisted retro, share the link with your team, and delete it when you're done.

## Important: this app requires a browser

Free Retro is a client-rendered single-page application. If you arrived here via a plain HTTP fetch, you are seeing an empty HTML shell. To interact with the app, you must use a browser that executes JavaScript.

The app exposes a full agent API via WebMCP (`document.modelContext`) and a convenience wrapper (`window.freeretro`) once a board page is loaded in a browser.

## Quickstart for agents

1. Open a browser and navigate to https://freeretro.ziki.workers.dev
2. Create a retro via the UI or the REST API (see below)
3. Navigate to the retro URL: https://freeretro.ziki.workers.dev/retro/<id>
4. Once the board loads, call `window.freeretro.help()` in the browser console for full usage

## REST API

All retros are unlisted. There is no public listing or browse endpoint.

### Create a retro

POST /api/retros
Content-Type: application/json

{ "title": "Sprint 42 Retro", "createdBy": "agent" }

Response 201:
{ "id": "<uuid>", "title": "Sprint 42 Retro", "createdAt": 1234567890000, "createdBy": "agent" }

### Get a retro

GET /api/retros/:retroId

Response 200: RetroSummary object, or 404 if not found.

### Rename a retro

PUT /api/retros/:retroId
Content-Type: application/json

{ "title": "New Title" }

### Delete a retro

DELETE /api/retros/:retroId

Response: { "ok": true }

### Connect via WebSocket

GET /api/ws/:retroId
Upgrade: websocket

Optional query params:
- userId: stable identifier for this agent session
- name: display name shown to other users (default: "Anonymous")

On connection the server sends a full `state` snapshot, then broadcasts incremental updates.

## Browser agent API (requires JavaScript execution)

Once a retro board is open in a browser, the following are available:

### window.freeretro

- `window.freeretro.help()` - print full usage to the console
- `window.freeretro.tools` - array of all available tool descriptors
- `window.freeretro.call(name, args)` - call a tool by name
- `window.freeretro.getMode()` / `setMode('human'|'direct')` - interaction mode
- `window.freeretro.moveCursor(x, y)` - move the visible cursor
- `window.freeretro.isEmbodied()` / `setEmbodied(value)` - show/hide agent cursor

### document.modelContext (WebMCP)

- `document.modelContext.executeTool(name, args)` - call any tool directly

### Available tools

- list_cards - list all cards with column, author, content, upvote count, reactions
- list_columns - list columns with labels and card counts
- list_users - list connected users and agents
- get_board_state - blurred state, sort mode, counts
- create_card(columnId, content) - add a card; columnId is one of: highlights, challenges, questions, notes
- edit_card(cardId, content) - replace card text
- delete_card(cardId) - delete a card
- move_card(cardId, columnId, position?) - move a card to another column
- upvote_card(cardId) - toggle your upvote
- react_to_card(cardId, emoji) - toggle an emoji reaction
- rename_column(columnId, label) - rename a column
- set_blur(blurred) - blur or reveal all cards for everyone
- set_sort(sortByUpvotes) - sort by upvotes or restore manual order
- set_cursor(x, y) - move your visible cursor to viewport coordinates
- set_interaction_mode(mode) - "human" animates cursor so people can follow; "direct" applies changes instantly

## Interaction modes

- human (default): your cursor glides to each target so people watching can follow along
- direct: changes apply instantly without cursor movement

Switch with `window.freeretro.setMode('human')` or the `set_interaction_mode` tool.
